result.title: MotoBarna Website Facts

facts.text: The V1 MotoBarna website is a polished one-page landing site implemented as an Astro static site.
The first viewport on mobile and desktop presents MotoBarna as a Barcelona MotoGymkhana and motorcycle community, with a visible Telegram CTA.
The landing page explains MotoGymkhana as a technical motorcycle time-trial sport with cone courses, course walking, timed runs, clean gates, line choice, and penalties.
The landing page shows that MotoBarna is also a social motorcycle community with Sunday parking-lot hangs, coffee, bike chat, help from other riders, and people who can come watch first.
The landing page includes group rides and trips around Barcelona and Catalunya, including route references such as Costa Brava, Montserrat, Montseny, Sitges, or Collserola.
The exact parking-lot location is not published publicly; the page says the meeting pin is shared in Telegram.

The page includes real-photo slots for MotoGymkhana, hanging out, and trips, and may use placeholders until real community photos are available. Also there will be 2 youtube videos from past weekend trips in Catalonia
The site includes a language switch with at least English and Russian options.
The Russian-language content may state that the community is run by Russian-speaking expats in Barcelona.

The page includes SEO and AI-search foundations: unique title and meta description, canonical URL, sitemap, robots.txt, Open Graph metadata, visible local/entity copy, and structured data that matches visible content.

The page includes privacy-light analytics hooks for Telegram CTA clicks and key section views without collecting personal data. It must include Mixpanel analytics and tracking how many went to the telegram channel.
All Telegram CTA buttons point to one shared Telegram destination value, @motobarna, https://t.me/motobarna
The implementation passes build checks and includes responsive browser QA for mobile and desktop.
The implementation checks accessibility basics, including readable contrast, keyboard-accessible CTAs, useful image alt text, and touch-friendly CTA sizing.

result.factsMarkdown:
# Facts

- The V1 MotoBarna website is a polished one-page landing site implemented as an Astro static site.
- The first viewport on mobile and desktop presents MotoBarna as a Barcelona MotoGymkhana and motorcycle community, with a visible Telegram CTA.
- The landing page explains MotoGymkhana as a technical motorcycle time-trial sport with cone courses, course walking, timed runs, clean gates, line choice, and penalties.
- The landing page shows that MotoBarna is also a social motorcycle community with Sunday parking-lot hangs, coffee, bike chat, help from other riders, and people who can come watch first.
- The landing page includes group rides and trips around Barcelona and Catalunya, including route references such as Costa Brava, Montserrat, Montseny, Sitges, or Collserola.
- The exact parking-lot location is not published publicly; the page says the meeting pin is shared in Telegram.
- The page includes real-photo slots for MotoGymkhana, hanging out, and trips, and may use placeholders until real community photos are available. Also there will be 2 youtube videos from past weekend trips in Catalonia
- The site includes a language switch with at least English and Russian options.
- The Russian-language content may state that the community is run by Russian-speaking expats in Barcelona.
- The page includes SEO and AI-search foundations: unique title and meta description, canonical URL, sitemap, robots.txt, Open Graph metadata, visible local/entity copy, and structured data that matches visible content.
- The page includes privacy-light analytics hooks for Telegram CTA clicks and key section views without collecting personal data. It must include Mixpanel analytics and tracking how many went to the telegram channel.
- All Telegram CTA buttons point to one shared Telegram destination value, @motobarna, https://t.me/motobarna
- The implementation passes build checks and includes responsive browser QA for mobile and desktop.
- The implementation checks accessibility basics, including readable contrast, keyboard-accessible CTAs, useful image alt text, and touch-friendly CTA sizing.
